questions: Q1: Were all 8 implementation steps executed successfully?
Q2: Does the .npmignore mechanism work correctly with modern npm?
Q3: Does the publish workflow correctly implement OIDC Trusted Publishing without any token secrets?
Q4: Does the update mechanism correctly migrate from GitHub-direct to npm-based flow?
Q5: Are all verification checks passing?

answers.answer: Yes. All 8 steps were executed: (1) package.json metadata added, (2) .npmignore created, (3) InstallSource type extended, (4) check.ts npm functions added, (5) perform.ts install spec changed, (6) index.ts update orchestration rewritten, (7) help text updated, (8) publish.yml created. One deviation: the files field in package.json was augmented with negation patterns to handle test exclusion because npm 11 ignores root .npmignore when files is present.
evidence: npm pack --dry-run shows 73 files, no test files, dist/index.js present

answers.answer: The .npmignore file exists with the planned patterns but is effectively a no-op because npm 11 ignores root .npmignore when the files field is present in package.json. Test file exclusion is handled by negation patterns in the files array: ['dist', '!dist/**/*.test.js', '!dist/**/*.test.d.ts']. Verified via npm pack --dry-run showing zero test files.
evidence: Context7 npm docs confirm: 'If a files array is present in the package.json, then rules in .gitignore and .npmignore files from the root will be ignored'
npm pack --dry-run output: 73 files, no *.test.js or *.test.d.ts patterns present

answers.answer: Yes. The workflow at .github/workflows/publish.yml triggers on tag push v*, sets permissions.id-token: write and contents: read, uses actions/setup-node with registry-url, runs npm ci + npm test, validates tag-version match, packs and inspects the tarball for dist/index.js presence and test file absence, then publishes the validated tarball with --provenance. No NPM_TOKEN or NODE_AUTH_TOKEN references exist.
evidence: File content inspection confirms all required elements
grep -i NPM_TOKEN|NODE_AUTH_TOKEN returns no matches (exit code 1)

answers.answer: Yes. The update flow now uses fetchLatestVersion() (npm view) instead of fetchRemoteSha() (git ls-remote), isNewerVersion() for semver comparison instead of SHA comparison, NPM_PACKAGE@latest as the install spec instead of github: spec, and saves config with mode:'npm' and version field. isCanonicalSource() accepts both 'github' (legacy) and 'npm' modes for backward compatibility.
evidence: src/update/perform.ts imports NPM_PACKAGE, installSpec is NPM_PACKAGE@latest
src/update/index.ts uses fetchLatestVersion + isNewerVersion, saves mode:'npm'
npm run typecheck passes with all changes

answers.answer: All 12 verification checks pass or are verified. CHK-01 (typecheck): pass. CHK-02 (tests): pass, 30/30. CHK-03 (metadata): pass. CHK-04 (pack): pass, 73 files, no test files. CHK-05 (version): pass, 1.2.0. CHK-06 (help): pass, shows 'npm'. CHK-07 (workflow): pass via file inspection. CHK-08 (InstallSource): pass. CHK-09 (check.ts): pass. CHK-10 (perform.ts): pass. CHK-11 (index.ts): pass. CHK-12 (.npmignore): pass, file exists with patterns.
evidence: All command outputs captured showing exit code 0
All file inspections confirm expected content

statement: Implementation complete. All 8 steps from the implementation plan were executed. package.json has repository and publishConfig metadata with test-file exclusion via files field negation patterns. .npmignore exists for documentation. InstallSource type extended with npm mode and version field. Update mechanism migrated from GitHub-direct to npm-based (fetchLatestVersion, isNewerVersion, NPM_PACKAGE@latest). Help text updated. Publish workflow created with OIDC permissions, tarball validation, and provenance. One deviation: test exclusion mechanism uses files field negation instead of .npmignore alone due to npm 11 behavior. All 12 verification checks pass.
